rulings.Case Title: CORY OBERDORFER VS. TURN("THE UTILITY REFORM NETWORK"), A NON PROFIT B et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 5, 2009, line 2, PLAINTIFF CORY OBERDORFER MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion denied. Plaintiff fails to maintain his summary adjudication burden by failing to demonstrate the amount of his damages. Supplemental briefing does not alter the Court's decision. To grant summary adjudication, plaintiff must present evidence regarding each element of his cause of action and completely dispose of the cause of action. (CCP sec. 437c(f)(1) and (p)1. Summary adjudication may be granted for a plaintiff where the damages are ascertainable and certain, thus avoiding a trial. Here, trial would not be avoided. Plaintiff fails to shift the burden on issues 10 and 11 because he does not show he complied with defendant's grievance process. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: REBECCA B. MANCE VS. STATE FARM INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 5, 2009, line 5, DEFENDANT THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to the ninth cause of action for dangerous condition. Plaintiff failed to file a claim. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. Demurrer sustained with leave to amend the sixth, seventh, and eighth causes of action. Claim may not have accrued until February 11, 2009 when plaintiff became aware of the extent of her injuries. Plaintiff may still be able to file a late claim with the City and County of San Francisco. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARILYN YU VS. COMMUNITY EDUCATIONAL SERVICES OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 5, 2009, line 6, PLAINTIFF MARILYN YU DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT.
